source_text.text:
[PDF_PAGE 30 | score=15] Dragons Dragons are an ancient, winged reptilian race. They are known and feared for their size, physical prowess, and magical abilities. The 10 sub- species of dragons are identified by the color of their scales. Each sub- species has 12 age categories. The oldest dragons are among the most powerful creatures in the world. Dragons range in size from several feet upon hatching to more than 100 feet after they have attained the status of great wyrm. The exact size varies according to age and subspecies. A dragon's wing span is about equal to its body length and fully 15-20% of a dragon's body length is its neck. Dragons, especially older ones, are generally solitary due to necessity and prefe
[PDF_PAGE 64 | score=14] Golems, Lesser Flesh Clay DIET: Nil INTELLIGENCE: Semi- (2-4) TREASURE: Nil ALIGNMENT: Neutral Neutral SPECIAL ATTACKS: Nil SPECIAL DEFENSE: See below MAGIC RESISTANCE: Nil Nil SIZE: L (7!/2' tall) MORALE: Fearless (19-20) XP VALUE: 5000 8000 A golem is an artificial humanoid body which is animated by an elemental spirit and under the control of its creator. Flesh Golem The flesh golem stands a head and a half taller than most humans and weighs almost 350 pounds. It is made from a ghoulish collec- tion of stolen human body parts, stitched together to form a sin- gle composite human body. Its skin is the sickly green or yellow of partially decayed flesh. A flesh golem smells faintly of freshl
[PDF_PAGE 2 | score=13] How To Use This Book The new Monstrous Compendium format was designed with the Dungeon Master in mind. Monster entries are given in alphabetical order, printed on looseleaf sheets that can be organized for convenience. The sheets needed for an adventure can be placed in a separate folder for play, then later returned to the master binder. New monsters can be easily placed in the master binder. All monsters described here are typical for their type; likewise, the given encounter tables are guidelines for general play. DMs should note that unusual variations are encouraged, but are most effective when they depart from the expected. Each monster is described fully, with entries that describe be
[PDF_PAGE 45 | score=13] Air/Earth Elementals Air Earth DIET: Air INTELLIGENCE: Low (5-7) TREASURE: Nil Nil ALIGNMENT: Neutral Earth, metal, or gem Low (5-7) Neutral SPECIAL ATTACKS: SPECIAL DEFENSES: See below +2 weapon or better to hit See below +2 weapon or better to hit MAGIC RESISTANCE: Nil Nil SIZE: L to H (8' to 16’ tall) L to H (8' to 16’ tall) MORALE: 8-12 Hit Dice: Champion 8-12 Hit Dice: Champion (15-16) (15-16) 16 Hit Dice: Fanatic (17) 16 Hit Dice: Fanatic (17) XP VALUE: 8 Hit Dice: 3,000 8 Hit Dice: 2,000 12 Hit Dice: 7,000 12 Hit Dice: 6,000 16 Hit Dice: 11,000 Air elementals can be conjured in any area of open air where gusts of wind are present. The common air elemental appears as an amorphous, shif
[PDF_PAGE 46 | score=12] Fire/Water Elementals Fire Water DIET: INTELLIGENCE: Any combustible Low (5-7) ‘TREASURE: Nil Nil ALIGNMENT: Neutral Any liquid Low (5-7) Neutral SPECIAL ATTACKS: See below SPECIAL DEFENSES: +2 weapon or better to hit MAGIC RESISTANCE: Nil Nil SIZE: L to H (8' to 16’ tall) 8-12 Hit Dice: Champion (15-16) 16 Hit Dice: Fanatic (17) 8 Hit Dice: 2,000 12 Hit Dice: 6,000 16 Hit Dice; 10,000 MORALE: XP VALUE: Fire elementals can be conjured in any area containing a large open flame. To provide a fire elemental with an adequate shell of Prime Material flame, a fire built to house an elemental should have a diameter of at least six feet and reach a minimum of four feet into the air. On the Prime Mat
[PDF_PAGE 55 | score=12] Giant, Fire DIET: INTELLIGENCE: Omnivorous Low to average (5-10) TREASURE: E ALIGNMENT: Lawful evil SPECIAL ATTACKS: Hurling rocks for 2-20 (2d10) SPECIAL DEFENSES: Resistant to fire MAGIC RESISTANCE: Nil SIZE: H (18') MORALE: Champion (15-16) XP VALUE: 8,000 Infant Nil Juvenile, -3 120 Juvenile, -2 3,400 Juvenile, -1 5,000 Shaman/Witch doctor, Ist 9,000 Shaman/Witch doctor, 2nd 10,000 Shaman/Witch doctor, 3rd 10,000 Shaman/Witch doctor, 4th 12,000 Fire giants are brutal, ruthless, and militaristic. They are tall, but squat, resembling huge dwarves. An adult male is 18’ tall, has a 12' chest, and weighs about 7,500 pounds. Fire giants have coal black skin, flaming red or bright orange hair, 
[PDF_PAGE 65 | score=12] Golems, Greater Stone Iron DIET: INTELLIGENCE: Non- (0) Non- (0) TREASURE: Nil Nil ALIGNMENT: Neutral Neutral SPECIAL ATTACKS: See below See below SPECIAL DEFENSES: See below See below MAGIC RESISTANCE: Nil Nil SIZE: L (9%/2' tall) L (12' tall) MORALE: Fearless (19-20) Fearless (19-20) XP VALUE: 10,000 15,000 A greater golem is an artificial humanoid body which has been animated by an elemental spirit but remains under the complete control of its creator, Stone Golem A stone golem is 9/2 feet tall, and weighs around 2000 pounds. Its body is of roughly chiseled stone, frequently stylized to suit its creator. For example it might be carved to to look like it is wear- ing armor with a particula
[PDF_PAGE 72 | score=12] Lernaean Carnivore INTELLIGENCE: Semi- (2-4) TREASURE: Nil ALIGNMENT: Neutral Semi- (2- 4) Semi- (2-4) Nil Nil Neutral Neutral SPECIAL ATTACKS: Nil G (30' long) Average (8-10) XP VALUE: 2000 3000 Heads THACO Damage 5 15 1-6 6 13 1-6 7 13 1-8 8 12 1-8 Hydrae are immense reptilian monsters with multiple heads. For each hit die which the hydra has it will have one head. The chart above lists the THACO value for hydrae with a given number of heads and the damage which they inflict each time they bite a foe. Hydrae are gray-brown to dark brown, with lighter, yellow or tan un- derbellies. Their eyes are amber and their teeth are yellow-white. Hydrae will have between 5 and 12 heads (1d8 +4), Comba
